title: Natural Relief for Abdominal Pain: Top 9 Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for abdominal pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relax the muscles in the stomach, providing relief from cramps and discomfort.
Shogaol is good for abdominal pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the stomach.
Curcumenol is good for abdominal pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the stomach and intestines.
Zingerone is good for abdominal pain because it reduces inflammation and relaxes the muscles in the abdomen, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing digestive discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a natural remedy for abdominal pain. Ginger contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the stomach and intestines. When applied topically as a cream, gingerol can penetrate the skin and reach the affected area, providing quick relief from cramps, spasms, and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory action of ginger helps to calm down the digestive system, reducing the severity of symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, ginger has natural antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the abdominal wall and prevent spasms that can cause pain. By applying ginger cream to the affected area, you may find that your symptoms improve and you feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with abdominal pain, first, you need to dry the plant. You can dry it by tying the stems together and hanging them upside down in a warm, dark place. This process usually takes 2-3 weeks. Once the leaves are dry, you can make a herbal tea by steeping 1 tablespoon of dried Angelica in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool down, then strain the liquid. Combine the cooled liquid with 1 cup of coconut oil or olive oil in a saucepan and heat it gently over low heat. As the mixture warms up, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Remove from heat and let it cool down completely. Strain the liquid again and discard the solids. Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the liquid and mix well. Pour the mixture into a jar and store it in the fridge. To use the cream, apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream is good for abdominal pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties. Curcuma longa is a plant that contains a chemical called curcumin. Curcumin helps reduce swelling and pain in the body. When you apply the cream to your abdomen, it absorbs into the skin and reaches the underlying tissues. The curcumin in the cream then blocks the production of chemicals that cause pain and inflammation. This helps to calm down the abdominal muscles and reduce the pain. The cream also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the tissues from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, the pain and discomfort in the abdomen start to subside. The cream can be especially helpful for people who suffer from conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), where abdominal pain is a common symptom.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for abdominal pain, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Angelica root, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. Begin by infusing the dried Angelica root in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by placing the root in a clean glass jar and covering it with the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally to help the herb infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once melted, add the infused oil, vitamin E oil, and any other desired essential oils to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine, then remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Once thickened, transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in a cool, dark place. When needed, apply a small amount of the Angelica archangelica cream to the affected area and gently massage it in to help alleviate abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are made from the root of the licorice plant. They contain a powerful compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to soothe and calm the stomach. When we eat, the stomach acids and digestive juices can sometimes get out of balance, causing pain and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin in the creams works by reducing the production of stomach acids, which helps to ease the pain and inflammation in the abdominal area. It also helps to protect the stomach lining from damage, which can be caused by excess acids and digestive juices. By reducing the irritation and inflammation in the stomach, the creams can help to provide quick relief from abdominal pain and discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for abdominal pain, you will need dried Angelica root or fresh Angelica leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a herbal infusion pot, cheesecloth, a mixing bowl, and a cream base like beeswax or shea butter. Start by making a strong herbal infusion by placing the dried Angelica root or fresh leaves in the herbal infusion pot, and then adding a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to cover the herbs. Heat the mixture gently over low heat for about 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their healing properties into the oil. Once the infusion has cooled, strain it through cheesecloth to remove the herbs, and then discard the solids. Next, melt the cream base in a separate bowl over low heat, and then mix it with the infused oil in a 1:1 ratio to create the final cream. Stir the mixture thoroughly until it is smooth and even, and then pour it into a clean glass jar or container. Allow the cream to cool and harden completely before using it on the affected area to help alleviate abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint oil, is often found in creams used to treat abdominal pain. The active compound in peppermint, menthol, helps to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines. This relaxation reduces spasms and cramps that can cause abdominal pain. The cooling effect of menthol also helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation. Additionally, peppermint oil can help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). When applied topically as a cream, the menthol is absorbed into the skin and then into the bloodstream, where it can reach the muscles and tissues in the abdominal area. This can provide quick relief from abdominal pain, including cramps, bloating, and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams for abdominal pain, start by gathering dried Angelica root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a base cream. First, mix 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica root with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes to make a tea. Strain the tea and let it cool down. Once cooled, mix the tea with the carrier oil in a bowl, and let it sit for 2-3 hours to allow the mixture to infuse. After the infusion, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, and discard the solids. Next, add a base cream to the liquid mixture and mix well until it's fully incorporated. Transfer the mixture into a clean jar and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. When you need to use the cream, simply scoop out a small amount and apply it to the affected area. Note: It's recommended to do a patch test on a small area of skin before using the cream to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the Angelica archangelica.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for abdominal pain because it has healing properties. The cream contains a gel-like substance called aloe vera, which has been used for centuries to treat skin problems and injuries. When you apply aloe barbadensis cream to your stomach area, it helps to soothe the skin and reduce inflammation. This can provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by abdominal issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, or stomach ulcers. The cream also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and ease cramps. Additionally, aloe barbadensis cream can help to calm the digestive system and promote the healing of damaged tissues in the stomach and intestines. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms like bloating, nausea, and stomach cramps, making it a natural remedy for abdominal pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for abdominal pain relief, start by gathering dried Angelica root and making a strong tea by steeping 2 tablespoons of the root in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid and let it cool down. Next, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan and heat it gently until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add 1 cup of the cooled Angelica tea, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens into a smooth cream. Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops of lavender essential oil for its soothing properties. Once cooled, the cream can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the abdomen and gently massage it in, allowing the anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica archangelica to help ease abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant with many health benefits. The cream made from its seeds has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including abdominal pain. This is because fennel has natural compounds that can soothe and calm the digestive system. When taken internally, fennel seeds can help to ease bloating and gas, which can often lead to abdominal pain. The cream, applied topically to the affected area, can help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps. It is believed that fennel's anti-inflammatory properties are responsible for its pain-relieving effects, making it a popular natural remedy for abdominal discomfort. The cream can be applied as needed, and it is generally considered safe for use by people of all ages.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for abdominal pain relief, you will need some dried Angelica root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a cream base or a beeswax. First, start by preparing the Angelica root by cleaning and drying it thoroughly. Then, steep 1-2 tablespoons of the dried root in 1 cup of the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily to help the oil extract the medicinal properties. After the steeping process, strain the mixture and discard the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with the cream base or melt the beeswax in a double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil while stirring constantly. Continue to heat and stir until the mixture thickens and forms a smooth cream. Finally, let the cream cool, then transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to preserve its potency and effectiveness. Apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area to help soothe and ease abdominal pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with abdominal pain. The main active ingredient, quinine, is found in the Cinchona tree. Quinine is a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and discomfort in the abdominal area. When applied topically as a cream, it can ease pain and cramps caused by menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and other abdominal problems. Quinine also has antispasmodic properties, which help relax the muscles in the abdomen and stop spasms. This can provide quick relief from cramps and discomfort. The cream is also non-irritating and gentle on the skin, making it a safe option for daily use.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with abdominal pain, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Angelica root, coconut oil, beeswax, a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, vitamin E oil, and essential oils like peppermint or ginger. Begin by making a herbal infusion: combine 1 tablespoon of dried Angelica root with 1 cup of boiling water, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Next, strain the liquid and discard the solids. In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax. Once melted, add 1 tablespoon of the herbal infusion, 1 teaspoon of the carrier oil, and 1/2 teaspoon of vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into a clean, sterilized container. Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area, ideally after a warm bath or shower to help relax the muscles.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The cream made from its seeds is often used to relieve abdominal pain. The active compound in Silybum marianum is silymarin, which helps to protect the liver and reduce inflammation. When the liver is healthy, it can function properly, and toxins are removed from the body efficiently. This can help to reduce abdominal pain caused by liver problems, such as gallstones or hepatitis. Additionally, Silybum marianum cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce pain. It also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the cells from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Silybum marianum cream can help to provide relief from abdominal pain. It is often used in combination with other natural remedies, such as ginger and peppermint, to enhance its effects. Silybum marianum cream can be applied topically to the affected area, and it is also available in capsule or tablet form for oral consumption.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for abdominal pain, start by gathering dried Angelica root, which can be purchased at a health food store or online. Begin by making a herbal infusion by steeping one tablespoon of dried Angelica root in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Next, mix one-quarter cup of beeswax with one-quarter cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax and coconut oil have melted, slowly add the cooled Angelica infusion to the mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened, then transfer it to a glass jar. remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica cream is often used to help with abdominal pain. This cream comes from the root of the angelica plant, which has been used in herbal medicine for a long time. The active ingredients in the cream, such as ferulic acid and other compounds, have anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to the skin, these ingredients can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the abdominal area. The cream may also help to relax the muscles in the abdomen and improve digestion. Some people find that using Angelica archangelica cream helps to calm their stomach and reduce cramping, bloating, and discomfort. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area to help soothe and calm the skin and underlying tissues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica archangelica herbal cream that may help with abdominal pain, start by harvesting fresh or dried roots and leaves of the plant. Dry them in a warm, well-ventilated area or in a low-temperature oven. Once dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1 part powder to 3 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly, until the powder dissolves into the oil. Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool. Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar. Add a natural emulsifier like beeswax or shea butter to the cream to help it thicken and maintain its texture. Finally, mix well and store the cream in the refrigerator to prolong its shelf life. To use the cream, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area, ideally after a warm bath or shower when the skin is most receptive.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for abdominal pain

intro_desc: Abdominal pain can be a real nuisance, making it hard to enjoy daily activities. Using herbal creams can be a great way to find relief. Two key herbs found in these creams are Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Curcuma longa, or turmeric. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the abdominal area. It also has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate cramps and discomfort. Turmeric, on the other hand, contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract. By using creams that contain these herbs, you can find relief from abdominal pain and get back to your normal activities. Abdominal pain is not just a minor issue - it can be a sign of a more serious problem, such as a digestive disorder or a food intolerance. If left unchecked, it can lead to digestive problems, fatigue, and even depression. By addressing the issue, you can improve your overall quality of life and feel more energetic and confident.
supplementary_best: To remedy abdominal pain, a combination of herbal creams can be effective. The best combination would be Zingiber officinale (Ginger) and Foeniculum vulgare (Fennel) with a base of Aloe barbadensis (Aloe Vera). Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the abdominal area. Fennel, on the other hand, has a natural relaxant effect that soothes the digestive system and eases cramps. Aloe Vera provides a soothing and moisturizing base to the cream, allowing the other herbs to penetrate deeper into the skin. This combination targets the root causes of abdominal pain, including inflammation and digestive issues, making it an effective remedy. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginger and the relaxant effect of Fennel work together to provide fast relief from abdominal pain, while the Aloe Vera base helps to calm and moisturize the skin, promoting a speedy recovery.
supplementary_avoid: If you have abdominal pain, there are some herbal creams to avoid. One of these is cream containing 'Equisetum arvense' (horsetail). Horsetail contains silicic acid, which can irritate the digestive system and make your abdominal pain worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Valeriana officinalis'. While valerian root can help with sleep, it can also slow down digestion, which can worsen abdominal pain. 'Vitex agnus-castus' (chasteberry) cream can also cause problems. Chasteberry can stimulate the uterus, which can cause stomach cramps and make abdominal pain worse. Lastly, creams containing 'Cimicifuga racemosa' (black cohosh) should be avoided. Black cohosh can also slow down digestion and cause stomach cramps, making abdominal pain worse. It's best to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal creams, especially if you have a pre-existing condition or are taking medication.
supplementary_other_remedies: Applying heat to the abdominal area with a warm compress or heating pad can help alleviate pain by increasing blood flow to the affected area. This is because heat relaxes the muscles and reduces inflammation, which can contribute to pain. Some people find relief by taking a warm bath or using a hot water bottle wrapped in a towel to avoid direct contact with the skin.  Another natural remedy is to try the "5-15-30" breathing technique, which involves taking deep breaths in through the nose for a count of five, holding the breath for a count of 15, and exhaling through the mouth for a count of 30. This technique can help calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate abdominal pain.  Drinking plenty of water can also help alleviate abdominal pain by flushing out toxins and keeping the digestive system functioning properly. Some people find that sipping on ginger tea or peppermint tea can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and discomfort.  Applying gentle pressure to the abdominal area with a tennis ball or rolling massager can also help stimulate digestion and relieve pain. This is because gentle pressure can help stimulate the muscles in the abdominal wall to move food through the digestive system more efficiently.  It's worth noting that some people find relief from abdominal pain by practicing gentle yoga or stretching exercises, such as child's pose or cat-cow stretches, to help relax the muscles and improve digestion. These remedies should not replace medical treatment, but can be used in conjunction with other therapies to help manage symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have abdominal pain often experience other issues related to their digestive system. One common problem is bloating, which is when the stomach feels tight and uncomfortable. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger can help alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach.  Another issue is constipation, where bowel movements become less frequent or harder to pass. Herbs like senna and licorice root can help stimulate the digestive system and make bowel movements easier.  Some people with abdominal pain may also experience diarrhea, where stool becomes loose and watery. Herbs like aloe vera and slippery elm can help soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Inflammation in the digestive tract, known as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), can also cause abdominal pain. Herbs like turmeric and boswellia can help reduce inflammation and calm the digestive system, providing relief from IBS symptoms.  Finally, some people may experience nausea and vomiting, which can be caused by abdominal pain. Herbs like ginger and lavender can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making it easier to keep food down.
